data.narrative:
Scout Space is a Series A-stage space and aerospace company founded by Philip Hover-Smoot that has raised $18M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 5 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers in the same sector, including ClearSpace, Hubble Network, Aalyria, and Sift.

Two valuation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression, yielding $36M. The peer-median method anchors to a median valuation of $400M across 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables (ClearSpace at $400M Series A, Hubble Network at $400M Series B, Sift at $274M Series B, and Aalyria at $1.3B Series B), establishing a range of $274M to $1.3B. Public comp anchoring is not applicable due to absence of disclosed revenue for the target.

The triangulated analysis across these two methods supports a valuation range of $31M (low) to $460M (high), with a median of $218M. Material uncertainties include the wide dispersion across peer valuations—spanning from $274M to $1.3B—and the absence of revenue data that would enable public market cross-checks. The stage-progression floor reflects only cash-in, while the peer-median ceiling reflects mature Series B activity in the sector.
